questions.question: Study the given pattern carefully and select the number that can replace the question mark (?) in it. (310, 45, 240) (892, 213, 486) (?, 172, 193)
Select the option which is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term. Obese: Fat :: ? : Thin
questions.explanation: Synonym of Obese is Fat So, Synonym of Thin is Slender

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: All the given except Brazil are European countries.

questions.question: In the following question, three statements are given and three conclusions are given below these statements. Choose the option which shows the conclusions which logically follow from the given statements, disregarding the commonly known facts. Statements: All Jokes are Puns No Puns is Tales All Tales are Stories Conclusions: I. Some Stories can be Puns II. No Story is Puns III. Some Jokes being Tales is a possibility
options: Both conclusions I and III follow
questions.explanation: Statements: All Jokes are Puns No Puns is Tales All Tales are Stories ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26215/image003.png) Some Stories can be Puns: No Puns is Tales. All Tales are Stories. The Story which is not Tales can be Puns. So, conclusion I is true. No Story is Puns: All Jokes are Puns. No Puns is Tales. All Tales are Stories. Thus, Some Story is Tales is not Puns So, conclusion II is false. Some Jokes being Tales is a possibility: All Jokes are Puns. No Puns is Tales. Thus, No Jokes is Tales. So, conclusion III is false. Hence, only conclusion I follows.

questions.question: You come to know that one of your teenage nephews (16 years old) owns a fake identity card which has a different name and mentions him to be 21 years old. Which of the following courses of action will be adopted by you?
options: Send him to a juvenile correctional facility
Ask him to stop using the fake identity-card
Suggest him safe clubs in the city which he can visit
Seize the fake card and tell his parents about it
questions.explanation: Asking him to stop using it may not actually make him stop using it. He has lied before and got a fake i-card, so he might do it again. Hence, the card should be seized and the parents should be alerted about what he has done (or might have done using the card like visiting places where minors are not allowed or drinking, etc). He should be counselled and allowed to talk about why he felt the need to forge the identity of an adult while he is still a minor.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the sixth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number 89 : 183 :: 61 : 127 :: 72 : ?
In the following series, one of the pairs of numbers does not follow the given pattern. Choose the odd pair out. 87-54, 88-66, 89-70, 90-82, 91-7
Select the option that represents the letters that, when placed from left to right in the following blanks will complete the letter series. E K _ G U _ E _ S G _ X E M _ G W _
options: S, X, K, V, T, X
S, X, L, V, S, X
S, X, M, V, S, X
S, X, L, V, T, X
questions.explanation: Here there is an alphabetical series, 1st - K, L, M 2nd - U, V, W E K S G U X E L S G V X E M S G W X So, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: If 'P1Q' means 'P is the father of Q', 'P2Q' means 'P is the brother of Q', 'P3Q' means 'P is the husband of Q', 'P4Q' means 'P is the sister of Q', 'P6Q' means 'P is the wife of Q' and 'P5Q' means 'P is the daughter of Q'. Then which of the following expressions shows that F is the grandfather of J?
questions.explanation: Option (a): J1F: J is the father of F F3K: F is the husband of K K4L: K is the sister of L L5C: L is the daughter of C ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26066/image009.png) Here, F is not the grandfather of J whereas J is the father of F. Option (b): F1L: F is the father of L L6K: L is the wife of K K1J: K is the father of J ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26066/image010.png) Here, F is the grandfather of J. Hence this option is correct. Option (c): C6L: C is the wife of L L1J: L is the father of J J6K: J is the wife of K K1F: K is the father of F ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26066/image011.png) Here, F is not the grandfather of J. Hence, this option is incorrect. Option (d): L6K: L is the wife of K K1F: K is the father of F F2J: F is the brother of J J3C: J is the husband of C ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26066/image012.png) Here, F is not the grandfather of J. Hence, this option is incorrect. So, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What will be the next set of numbers in the series? (16, 25), (25, 34), (34, 43), (43, 52), (52, 61), ?
Which of the following word will be formed by arranging the letters given below? C M B R S L A E
questions.explanation: The word that can be formed is Scramble.

questions.question: Two sequences are interwoven in the series: 5, 2, 10, 4, 15, 6, ?, ?
questions.explanation: Odd-position terms: 5, 10, 15, .. (add 5) → next = 20 Even-position terms: 2, 4, 6, .. (add 2) → next = 8

questions.question: If HOUSE is coded as 86315 in a certain language, then how will SECTION be coded?
questions.explanation: If the number is single digit, it is written as it is If the number is 2-digit number, then the digits of the numbers are added until a single digit is obtained H : 8 =>8 O : 15 => 1+5=6 U : 21 => 2+1 = 3 S : 19 =>1+9 = 10 => 1+0 = 1 E : 5 => 5 Similarly S : 19 => 1+9 = 10 => 1+0 = 1 E : 5 => 5 C : 3 => 3 T : 20 => 2+0 =2 I : 9 O : 15 => 1+5=6 N : 14 => 1+4=5

questions.question: Select the term that will come next in the following series: d, ef, hij, mnop, _____
questions.explanation: d + 1 = e two terms written together and leave a gap of 1 ef + 2= h three terms written together and leave a gap of 2 hij + 3 = m four terms written together and leave a gap of 3 mnop +4= t five terms written together and leave a gap of 4

questions.question: Each question given below consists of a statement, followed by two arguments numbered I and II. You have to decide which of the following arguments is a strong argument and which is a weak argument. Statement: Should subsidies be granted for small businesses and startups? Arguments: I. Yes. It will generate employment in the country. II. No, there will be financial losses if businesses and startups fail.
options: If only argument II is strong.
If neither argument I nor II is strong.
If only argument I is strong.
If both arguments I and II are strong.
questions.explanation: Businesses and startups generate employment in the surrounding region and thus reduce the burden on the employment market. So, argument I is strong. Just because of the risk of failure in businesses and startups, we cannot stop working on it. Entrepreneurs help in the industrial development of the country. So, argument II is weak.

questions.question: Introducing Karina to the guest, Manav said "She is the only daughter of my mother's son-in-law". If Manav's mother have two children i.e., One male and one female then, how is Manav related to Karina?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25399/image007.png) So, Manav is the uncle of Karina.

questions.question: Select the option which can replace the question mark in the given series. 9, 10, ? , 23, 39, 64
questions.explanation: Logic for the given series- 9 + 12 = 10 10 + 22 = 14 14 + 32 = 23 23 + 42 = 39 39 + 52 = 64 Hence, missing term = 14

questions.question: What should come in place of question mark? A, C, F, J, O, ?
questions.explanation: Logic for the series: A + 2 = C C + 3 = F F + 4 = J J + 5 = O O + 6 = U Hence, the missing term is U.

questions.question: If Sq denotes '+', Km denotes ' X ', Hy denotes '-', and Wf denotes '÷', then find the value of the following expression. 108 Km (√196 Hy 9) Wf 60 Sq 247
questions.explanation: Sq denotes '+' Km denotes ' X ' Hy denotes '-' Wf denotes '÷' 108 X (√196 - 9) ÷ 60 + 247 = 108 X (14 - 9) ÷ 60 + 247 = 108 X 5 ÷ 60 + 247 = 9 + 247 = 256

questions.question: If in a certain language, EXECUTIVE is coded as TCIEUXVEE, then how is MAUSOLEUM coded in that language?
questions.explanation: Let us label the letters of the given word from 1 to 9. Then, the code contains the letters of the word in the order 6, 4, 7, 3, 5, 2, 8, 1, 9. Thus, we have: EXECUTIVE 123456789 TCIEUXVEE 647352819 MAUSOLEUM 123456789 LSEUOAUMM 647352819

questions.question: Study the information given below and answer the question given below. A %% B means that neither A and B are equal nor A is smaller than B A ?? B means that neither A is greater than B nor A is smaller than B A !! B means that neither A and B are equal nor A is greater than B If X ?? Z !! W %% Y, then which of the following conclusions is definitely right?
questions.explanation: It is given that, A %% B means that neither A and B are equal nor A is smaller than B i.e. A > B. A ?? B means that neither A is greater than B nor A is smaller than B i.e A = B. A !! B means that neither A and B are equal nor A is greater than B i.e. A < B. It is given that, X ?? Z !! W %% Y. It means that, X = Z < W > Y. Hence, W > X is definitely right.

questions.question: In the question, a statement is given and is followed by two conclusions. You have to take the given statement to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts, then select the proper alternative. Statement: Trying to curb your carbs instead of fats is the most effective way to lose belly fat. Conclusions: I: It is dangerous to lose fat. II: There is no other way to lose belly fat than to reduce the intake of carbs.
options: Either conclusion I or II follows
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: The statement does not specifically mention whether it is dangerous or not to lose belly fat and there could be other means to lose belly fat. Hence, both conclusions do not follow.

Looking at the photograph of a woman, Fiza said, "Her son's father is the brother of my husband's mother's only daughter-in-law." At whose photograph was Fiza looking?

questions.question: In a coded language, WORDS is coded as 23 15 18 4 19 then what will be the code for HEART ?
questions.explanation: Here, the positions of the letters are considered in the alphabetical order. While coding only the vowels of the words are considered. 12345678910111213 ABCDEFGHIJKLM 14151617181920212223242526 NOPQRSTUVWXYZ WORDS -- 23 15 18 4 19 Similarly, HEART -- 8 5 1 18 20

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides protection against double jeopardy?
questions.explanation: · Option B is correct because Article 20(2) prohibits prosecution and punishment more than once for the same offence. · It ensures legal safeguards following an acquittal or conviction. · It is part of the rights against conviction for offences (Article 20). · Option A is incorrect: Article 20(1) prohibits retrospective criminal laws. · Option C is incorrect: Article 21 protects life and personal liberty. · Option D is incorrect: Article 19(1)(g) guarantees freedom to practice any profession or trade.

questions.question: Which of the following projects are incorporated under PM Gati-Shakti Plan? a) Bharatmala b) Sagarmala c) UDAN Choose the correct option
questions.explanation: • The PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan includes a number of projects to improve India's infrastructure • It includes projects such as Bharatmala, Sagarmala, inland waterways, dry/land ports, and UDAN. Further Insight: • PM Gati Shakti - National Master Plan for Multi-modal Connectivity ○ Launched by the Prime Minister to streamline infrastructure connectivity. ○ A digital platform integrating 16 Ministries, including Railways and Roadways, for cohesive planning and implementation. ○ Key Objectives ■ Multi-modal Connectivity: • Provides integrated, seamless movement for people, goods, and services across various modes of transport. ■ Infrastructure Enhancement: • Facilitates last-mile connectivity and reduces travel time. • Improves the coordination and efficiency of infrastructure projects. ○ Vision of PM Gati Shakti ■ Incorporates Infrastructure Schemes: • Includes national and state projects like Bharatmala, Sagarmala, inland waterways, dry/land ports, UDAN, etc. ■ Enhanced Economic Zones: • Aims to boost connectivity in economic zones such as textile and pharmaceutical clusters, defense corridors, electronic parks, industrial and fishing clusters, and agricultural zones. • Promotes competitiveness of Indian businesses. ■ Technology Integration: • Leverages spatial planning tools using ISRO imagery developed by BiSAG-N (Bhaskaracharya National Institute for Space Applications and Geoinformatics) for advanced planning and monitoring.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are correct about the Purna Swaraj (Complete Independence) Resolution? I. It was adopted by the Indian National Congress at its Lahore session II. It was adopted in the year 1929
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. The Purna Swaraj (Complete Independence) Resolution was adopted by the Indian National Congress at its historic Lahore Session on December 31, 1929, under the presidency of Jawaharlal Nehru. This marked a significant shift in the Congress's position from demanding dominion status to complete independence from British rule.

questions.question: Which title is a short story collection, not a novel?
questions.explanation: Interpreter of Maladies by Jhumpa Lahiri is a short story collection that won the Pulitzer Prize (2000). The others are novels by Aravind Adiga (A), V. S. Naipaul (B), and Salman Rushdie (C). Further Insights: - Malgudi Days - R. K. Narayan; seminal Indian short stories. - Arranged Marriage - Chitra Banerjee Divakaruni; diasporic stories. - The White Tiger won the 2008 Man Booker Prize. - Midnight's Children won the Booker (1981) and "Booker of Bookers" twice (1993, 2008).

questions.question: Consider the following statement(s) in context of "Hindi Diwas". I. Hindi Diwas is celebrated every year on 15 September. II. On this day in 1949, the Hindi language was adopted as one of the official languages of the Republic of India. Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
questions.explanation: Indians on 14 September celebrate National 'Hindi Diwas'. On this day in 1949, the Hindi language was adopted as one of the official languages of the Republic of India. Written in the Devanagari script, the Constitution of India accepted Hindi language as the official language of India on 14 September 1949.

questions.question: Consider the Following Statement. I. Joint Meeting is Conducted by President as per Article 108. II. The Bill is deadlock after the Six Months. III. Chairperson of the Joint Sitting is Mandatorily Deputy Chairperson of Rajya Sabha. Which of the following is correct?
questions.explanation: In accordance with Article 108 of the Indian Constitution, the President of India convenes the joint session of the Parliament, which is presided over by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha or, in their absence, the Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha or, in their absence, the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha. Hence, it's not mandatory for the Deputy Chairperson of Rajya Sabha to chair the joint sitting.

questions.question: In February 2026, the Indian Air Force (IAF)conducted a joint in-situ air combat exercise in the Indian Ocean Region with which country?
questions.explanation: The Indian Air Force conducted a joint in-situ air combat exercise with the Royal Thai Air Force (RTAF) in the Indian Ocean Region from 9 - 12 February 2026, aimed at enhancing interoperability, operational coordination and defence cooperation. Further Insights The exercise involved extended maritime operations and mid-air refuelling. It reflects India's Act East Policy and deeper defence engagement with Southeast Asia. The IAF used AWACS and IL-78 tanker aircraft during the exercise.

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R): Assertion (A): The British used the Indo-Saracenic style in government buildings to show that they were the legitimate successors of the Mughal rulers. Reason (R): By using Indian features like domes and arches, the British tried to make their foreign rule look familiar, acceptable, and stable to Indians. Choose the correct answer:
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) correctly explains (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) does NOT explain (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is true. The British deliberately adopted Indo-Saracenic architecture to link themselves with earlier Indian rulers, especially the Mughals, and present their rule as legitimate. Reason (R) is also true. Using Indian architectural elements was a soft and psychological strategy to gain acceptance and reduce resistance to British rule. Why (R) explains (A): Because this "soft power" approach is exactly why the British chose Indo-Saracenic architecture - to appear rightful, familiar, and stable rulers. Further Insights Architecture was used as a political message, not just for beauty. Indo-Saracenic style mixed Indian and European designs. Buildings were meant to convey continuity of power.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the kingdoms of the Sangam Age: (a) The Pandyas, with their capital at Madurai, used the Tiger as their royal emblem. (b) The Cheras' royal insignia was the Bow and Arrow, indicating their martial prowess, and their primary port was Musiri. (c) The port of Poompuhar (Kaveripattanam) was under the control of the Cholas, whose symbol was the Fish. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (b) is correct. The Cheras' emblem was the Bow and Arrow, and Musiri was one of their major ports. Statement (a) is incorrect because the Pandyas' emblem was the Fish, not the Tiger. The Tiger was the symbol of the Cholas. Statement (c) is incorrect because while Poompuhar was a Chola port, their symbol was the Tiger, not the Fish. Further insights: Royal Insignias: Each dynasty had distinct royal emblems: · Pandyas: Two Fish (representing their control over sea trade) · Cholas: Tiger (symbolizing strength and valor) · Cheras: Bow and Arrow (indicating their martial prowess)KingdomsCapitalPortSymbol CherasVanjiThondi, MusiriBow and Arrow CholasUraiyurPoompuhar (Kaveripattanam)Tiger PandyasMaduraiKorkaiFish

questions.question: In 2023-24, according to a NITI Aayog assessment unveiled in September 2025, which of the following did NOT feature among the top three states by share of services in Gross State Value Added (GSVA)?
questions.explanation: The share of services in gross value added (GVA) in 2023-24 was the highest in Chandigarh (88.8 per cent), followed by Delhi (84.1 per cent), said a NITI Aayog report released last week. This indicates that a state or union territory (UT), while having high per capita income, is also more services oriented. The report only used 22 major states/UTs for the analysis. At the all -India level, the share of services in GVA stood at 54.5 per cent in 2023-24. In Chandigarh, the services sector is largely concentrated in 'trade & repair services' (35.4 per cent), followed by 'real estate, ownership of dwellings & professional services' (25.7 per cent), 'financial services' (15.9 per cent), 'public administration' (7.7 per cent), and 'other services' (6.8 per cent). 'The prominence of trade and repair reflects Chandigarh's zoning-led urban planning, which has enabled specialised retail and service corridors catering to the Tri-City region of Chandigarh'Mohali' Panchkula. Its dual-capital role sustains demand for professional services such as legal, architectural, and engineering services,' added the report. In Delhi as well, 'real estate, ownership of dwellings & professional services' contributed nearly 31 per cent, reflecting robust demand for commercial and consulting services. 'Financial services' contributed 19 per cent ' thus highlighting Delhi's status as a national financial centre with the 'transport & storage' sector contributing 13.7 per cent. This underscores its logistical importance. Among states, Karnataka has the highest share (62.3 per cent) of services in the gross state value added (GSVA), followed by Kerala (61.5 per cent) and Telangana (60.3 per cent). 'Real estate, ownership of dwellings & professional services' is the biggest contributor in the share of GVA of all these three states. Surprisingly, Bihar (58 per cent) also has a high share of services in the GSVA, despite having very low per capita income. For Bihar, trade & repair services' (27.8 per cent) and 'other services' (20 per cent), led by small-scale neighbourhood retail outlets and self-employment, were the biggest contributors. 'This divergence suggests that the services sector in the state may be primarily characterised by lower productivity and more informal segments,' the report notes. Meanwhile, Chhattisgarh (33.9 per cent) has the lowest share of services in the GSVA, due to the dominance of mining and mineral-based industries. The same is true for Odisha as services contribute only 38.2 per cent of the GSVA.' Despite urban centres such as Indore and Bhopal emerging as hubs for information technology (IT), education, and real estate, the share of services in the GVA of Madhya Pradesh (38.8 per cent) remains low. 'Lower-income states, unless they invest in the right enablers, risk being locked into stagnant or low-return service structures,' the report said. Gujarat (36.1 per cent), despite having high per capita income, has a low share of services in the GVA. This perhaps 'stems from a policy that emphasises on industrial and port-led growth.' The report by the NITI Aayog is among the first dedicated state-level assessments of output in the services sector, going beyond aggregate trends. It presents a disaggregated and multi-dimensional profile of the sector. The report concludes that services are less tied to location than manufacturing ' meaning that even geographically remote or landlocked states can benefit from this transformation. 'With the right investments in broadband, e-governance, and skill development, these states can leapfrog traditional industrial phases and move directly into modern, tradable, knowledge-based services,' it added.'

questions.question: The NISAR satellite is mainly designed to closely study which part of the Earth to monitor glacier movement and rising sea levels?
questions.explanation: NISAR stands for NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar (NISAR). It is a joint mission by NASA and Indian Space Research Organisation. One of its key objectives is to monitor: Glaciers Ice sheets Polar ice movement All these belong to the Cryosphere - the frozen part of Earth. Biosphere Biosphere means living organisms - plants, animals, humans. Hydrosphere Hydrosphere includes all water (oceans, rivers, lakes). But glaciers and ice sheets specifically belong to cryosphere. Lithosphere Lithosphere refers to Earth's crust and upper mantle (rocks and land). It deals with tectonics, Further Insights • NISAR uses dual-frequency Synthetic Aperture Radar (L-band and S-band). • It can monitor land deformation and earthquakes too. • It will help study climate change impacts. • Cryosphere melting is a major cause of global sea-level rise. • It will provide data every 12 days for most land and ice surfaces.

questions.question: Important sub-tributaries of the river Ganga is/are I. Chambal II. Betwa III. Ramganga IV. Kosi Select the answer using the codes given below.
questions.explanation: Ganga is one of the important rivers of the country. It traverses through states Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and West Bengal. Sub tributary of river is tributary of important tributaries of main river. The Yamuna, the Ramganga, the Ghaghara, the Gandak, the Kosi, the Mahananda and the Sone are the important tributaries of the Ganga. Rivers Chambal and Betwa are the important sub-tributaries, which join the Yamuna before it meets the Ganga. Source- India Yearbook

questions.question: Which cell organelle is incorrectly matched with its function?
options: Golgi apparatus - Protein modification and packaging.
questions.explanation: • Ribosomes are involved in protein synthesis, not lipid synthesis. • Lipid synthesis occurs in the smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER). Further Insight: Organelle NameFunction NucleusControls cell activities, contains genetic material (DNA), and regulates gene expression. It plays a key role in cell division and the replication of DNA. MitochondriaKnown as the "powerhouse of the cell." They generate ATP through aerobic respiration. They contain their own DNA and ribosomes, allowing them to self-replicate. RibosomesSites of protein synthesis. They translate mRNA to form specific polypeptides, functioning in the cytoplasm or on the Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum. Endoplasmic Reticulum (ER)Rough ER: Synthesizes proteins, Smooth ER: Synthesizes lipids and detoxifies harmful substances in liver cells. Golgi ApparatusModifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids for secretion or delivery to other organelles. Plays a role in the synthesis of glycoproteins and glycolipids. Lysosomes"Suicide bags" of the cell; they digest foreign substances and worn-out organelles using hydrolytic enzymes. Can trigger autolysis in damaged cells.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Yakshagana. I. It is a dance drama art form from Uttar Pradesh II. In this art form, the proponent uses only oratory skills Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?
questions.explanation: It is a dance-drama of Karnataka, it comprises of elaborate costumes, make ups and masks. Traditionally it is performed in open air by all male troupe while women also started taking part since 1970s. The narratives are drawn primarily from the Hindu epics like Ramayana, Mahabharata, youthful tales of Krishna as depicted in Bhagavata - purana. Talamaddale, a variant of Yakshagana is similar to Yakshagana without the costumes, dance. Source: The Hindu

questions.question: Which term is used in badminton for a shot that just clears the net and lands close to it?
questions.explanation: • "Drop Shot" refers to a soft shot that barely clears the net and falls close to it. • "Smash" refers to a powerful downward shot. • "Clear" is a shot that sends the shuttlecock to the back of the opponent's court. • "Drive" refers to a fast, flat shot in badminton. Further Insight: • Badminton Important Terminologies: ○ Smash - A powerful downward shot aimed at the opponent's court. ○ Drop Shot - A soft shot that falls just over the net. ○ Clear - A shot that sends the shuttlecock to the back of the opponent's court. ○ Rally - A sequence of shots between players before a point is won. ○ Drive - A fast and flat shot over the net. ○ Deuce - When both players have 20 points each, requiring a two-point lead to win.

questions.question: In November 2025, during the 43rd session of the UNESCO General Conference, Oman secured election to the International Coordinating Council of the Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme for the 2025-2029 term. The General Conference session where this milestone occurred was held in ________.
questions.explanation: The Sultanate of Oman has been elected to the UNESCO International Coordinating Council of the Man and the Biosphere Programme (MAB) for the 2025-2029 term. This major milestone occurred during the 43rd session of the UNESCO General Conference in Samarkand, Uzbekistan. By winning a seat on the MAB Council, Oman positions itself among a select group of nations that help steer global efforts in balancing human development with the conservation of nature. The election reflects Oman's growing reputation in environmental governance. The country's environmental authority, acting on behalf of the Sultanate, highlighted this achievement as a 'strategic step' in strengthening its role on global platforms. The MAB Programme, established in 1971, is designed to facilitate scientific research, innovation, ecosystem management and the meaningful integration of human and ecological systems. Member'states of its council help guide policy directions and programmes. As a council member for 2025-2029, Oman will: Contribute to shaping MAB policy, research priorities and funding mechanisms. Represent the needs and perspectives of the Arab/Asian region in biosphere management. Enhance its visibility in international environmental forums and strengthen partnerships. Help advance innovations and educational outreach in ecosystems where human and natural systems intersect. Boost its commitment to sustainable development and green growth, both at home and abroad.

questions.question: Ravi exchanged US$10 for Rs. 700 today, after 10 days, the cost of US$1 increased to Rs.75/-, which of the following best describes the situation?
questions.explanation: In this case, 1 USD today was valued at Rs. 70/-, it's price became Rs. 75/- after 10 days, in this case Indian rupee depreciated and USD appreciated. Initial Exchange Rate: Ravi exchanged US$1 for Rs. 700, indicating the value of the dollar relative to the rupee at that time. Change in Exchange Rate: After 10 days, the exchange rate changed to US$1 for Rs. 750. This means it now takes more rupees to buy the same amount of dollars. Appreciation of the Dollar: Since it takes more rupees to buy a dollar, it implies that the dollar has become more valuable or has appreciated compared to the rupee.

questions.question: Who was the last ruler of the Mauryan dynasty?
questions.explanation: · Option C is correct because Emperor Brihadratha was assassinated by Pushyamitra Shunga in 185 BCE, ending Mauryan rule. · His reign saw decentralization and weakening of central authority. · His overthrow marked the transition to the Shunga dynasty. · Option A is incorrect: Ashoka was the third Mauryan emperor. · Option B is incorrect: Bindusara was the second Mauryan emperor. · Option D is incorrect: Chandragupta Maurya was the dynasty's founder.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are not correct regarding Tropical deciduous forests?
options: They are also called as Monsoon forests
The mean annual temperature of this region is about 27 ͦ C
These are found in the areas with more than 200cm rainfall
Teak and Sal are the most important trees grown in this Forest
questions.explanation: Tropical deciduous forests: These are found in the areas with 100 to 200cm. annual rainfall. These are called 'Monsoon Forests'. The mean annual temperature of this region is about 27 ͦ C and the average annual relative humidity is 60 to 70 percent. The trees of these forests drop their leaves during the spring and early summer. Teak and Sal are the most important trees. Sandalwood, rosewood, kusum, mahua, palas, haldu, amla, padauk, bamboo and tendu are the other trees of economic importance. These forests also provide fragrant oil, varnish, sandal oil and perfumes.

questions.question: For a wave, _____ is the distance from the centre line (or the still position) to the top of a crest or to the bottom of a trough.
questions.explanation: · The amplitude of a wave is the distance from the centre line (or the still position) to the top of a crest or to the bottom of a trough. · Amplitude is measured in metres (m). · The greater the amplitude of a wave then the more energy it is carrying.

questions.question: Which Indian shuttler won the silver medal in women's singles at Rio 2016 Olympics?
questions.explanation: P. V. Sindhu defeated Saina Nehwal in the semi-final and won silver after losing to Carolina Marín. · First Indian woman to win an Olympic badminton medal. · Later became world champion in 2019.

questions.question: Consider the statements about removal of a judge of the supreme court and choose the correct option. I. A judge can be removed by the order of the President of India II. An address by the parliament should be presented to the President of India III. The address should be supported by a simple majority in both houses of parliament IV. A judge can be removed on the ground of proved incapacity only
options: Only I and II are correct
Only II and III are correct
Only I, III, IV are correct
I, II, III, IV are correct
questions.explanation: - A Judge of the Supreme Court shall not be removed from his office except by an order of the President passed after an address by each House of Parliament supported by a majority of the total membership of that House and by a majority of not less than two-thirds of the members of that House present and voting has been presented to the President in the same session for such removal on the ground of proved misbehaviour or incapacity.

questions.question: With reference to Indian polity, the famous 'Patel scheme' is related to which of the following?
questions.explanation: Sardar Patel played the most important role in the integration and merger of states. The main objective of shaping the Indian states into sizable or viable administrative units was sought to be achieved by three-fold process of integration, which is famously known as the "Patel scheme", after Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, the Minister-in-Charge of Home Affairs.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct about Net National Product? a) NNP calculated by adding depreciation from GNP. b) NNP is same as the National Income. c) NNP accounts for factor income from abroad. Choose the correct option
questions.explanation: • NNP is calculated by subtracting depreciation from GNP • NNP at factor cost is equivalent to National Income. • NNP refers to net domestic and foreign production but not in the same terms as factor income. Further Insight: • GNP = GDP + Net factor income from abroad • NNP (Net National Product): GNP (Gross National Product) adjusted for depreciation. ○ Formula: NNP=GNP−Depreciation • Gross National Income (GNI)at market prices = GDP at market prices + taxes less subsidies on production and imports (Net receivable from abroad) + Compensation of Employees (Net receivable from abroad) + property income (Net receivable from abroad) • Net National Income (NNI) at market prices = GNI at market prices - consumption of fixed capital • Net National Disposable Income(NNDI) = NNI + net taxes on income and wealth receivable from abroad + net social contributions and benefits receivable from abroad.

questions.question: What fraction bears the same ratio to 1_________115 as 5_________6 does to 3_________23 ?
questions.explanation: Let the fraction be x According to question, x : (1/115) = 5/6 : 3/23 x/(1/115) = (5/6)/(3/23) 115x = (5 * 23)/(3 * 6) x = 1/18

questions.question: Which one of the following triplets can be measures of side of a triangle?
questions.explanation: The triangle inequality theorem states that, in a triangle, the sum of lengths of any two sides is greater than the length of the third side. Checking by options: Option (a): 4 + 7 > 12 => 11 > 12 (False) Option (b): 5 + 6 > 13 => 11 > 13 (False) Option (c): 4.5 + 8.5 > 14.5 => 13 > 14.5 (False) Option (d): 7 + 10 > 16 => 17 > 16 (True) Hence, option d is correct.

questions.question: The ratio of the speed of two buses is 5: 6. If the second bus travels 480 km in 25 hours, then what is the speed of the first bus?
questions.explanation: Speed of second bus = 480/25 = 19.2 km/h According to question, 5/6 = speed of first bus / 19.2 => speed of first bus = 96/6 => speed of first bus = 16 km/h

questions.question: There are 2 towers with heights in the ratio 3:5, separated by a distance of 1400 m. From a point between the towers the angle of elevation made to the top of the shorter tower is 30° and to the top of the taller tower is 60°. What is the height of the shorter tower?
questions.explanation: Diagram not drawn to scale. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p5702/image005.jpg) AB and CD are towers. Let the lengths AB and CD be 3k metres and 5k metres respectively. In triangle ABE, tan 30° = AB/BE => BE = 3k√3 In triangle CED, tan 60° = CD/CE => CE = 5k/√3 = 5k√3/3 Now, 3k√3 + 5k√3/3 = 1400 => 9k√3 + 5k√3 = 4200 => k = 300/√3 = 100√3 Height of shorter tower = 3k = 300√3 m

questions.question: If K2 : M :: M : 16, then what is the value of M if K= 2?
In how much time will the amount of Rs.12550 double itself at a rate 8% simple interest per annum?
questions.explanation: For doubling an amount at given rate of simple interest, time required = 100/R Hence time required to double the amount = 100/8 = 12.5 years

questions.question: If Sec2 A + tan2 A = 4_________17 , then sec4 A - tan4 A is equal to :

questions.question: A sum of Rs. 90,000 is invested for 11⁄2 years at 8% p.a. compounded half-yearly. Find the compound interest.
questions.explanation: Compounding half-yearly n = 2 per year Rate per period r = 8/2 = 4% Total periods n = (3/2) x 2 = 3 Now, A = 90000 x (1 + 0.04)3 A = 90000 x (104 x 104 x 104)/1000000 A = 101,237.76 CI = A - P = 101,237.76 - 90000 = Rs.11237.76

questions.question: Which of the following expression (s) is/are true? I. √5+√5 > √2+2√2 II. √4+√7 > √2+√8 III. √6+√8 < √4+√12
questions.explanation: I. √5+√5 > √2+2√2 Squaring both side we get, 10+2√25 > 10+2√16 2√25 > 2√16 10 > 8 True II. √4+√7 > √2+√8 Squaring both side we get, 11+2√28 > 10+2√16 True III. √6+√8 < √4+√12 Squaring both side we get, 14+2√48 < 16+2√48 True

questions.question: When a number is divided successively by 9, 14, and 16, we get the remainder of 6, 9, and 5, respectively. When the same number is divided successively by 16, 14, and 9, we get the remainder of A, B, and C, respectively. Find the value of (A - B + C).
questions.explanation: Let in the first condition, the last quotient be 1. So, dividend for 16 = 16 * 1 + 5 = 21 Dividend for 14 = 14 * 21 + 9 = 303 Dividend for 9 = 9 * 303 + 6 = 2733 So, the number is 2733 Now, 2733/16, Quotient = 170 and Remainder (A) = 13 170/14, Quotient = 12 and remainder (B) = 2 12/9, Quotient = 1 and remainder (C) = 3 Hence, A - B + C = 13 - 2 + 3 = 14

questions.question: If m cot2 30° - m_________1 + tan2 60° = 4 cosec2 90° - sec2 30° + cos2 30°, then what is the value of m (correct to two decimal places)?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23079/image007.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23079/image008.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23079/image009.png) => m/2 = 41/12 => m = 41/6 => m = 6.83

questions.question: A building in shape of a pyramid is to be painted on the outer walls at a rate of Rs.160 per square metre. If slant height of the building is 5m and the base dimension of the pyramid is 4m X 4m, find the cost of painting the building.
questions.explanation: Area of a pyramid = PL/2 Where, P = perimeter of base = 4*4m = 16m Area to be painted = (16 X 5)/2 = 40 m2 Cost of painting = 40 X 160 = Rs.6400

questions.question: Two partners invest equally. At year-end, they donate 8% of the profit to charity, then split the remainder equally. If each partner's share is ₹46,000, what was the total profit?
questions.explanation: Let the total profit be Rs. P After donation = 92% of P = 0.92P Each partner's share = ½ X 0.92P 46,000 = 0.46P Hence, P = 4600/0.46 = ₹100,000

questions.question: What will be the total cost (in Rs.) of painting the curved surface area of a metal cylinder at the rate of Rs.10 per sq. cm, if its diameter is 126 cm and height is 8 cm?
questions.explanation: Diameter = 126 cm Radius = 126/2 cm Height = 8 cm Curved surface area of cylinder = 2 * π * radius * height = 2 * (22/7) * (126/2) * 8 = 22 * 18 * 8 = 3168 sq. cm Required cost = 3168 * 10 = Rs.31680

questions.question: A hollow cylindrical pipe is 20 cm long, with an external radius of 5 cm and a uniform thickness of 1 cm. Find the total surface area of the pipe to the nearest integer. Use π = 3.14.
questions.explanation: · External radius (R) = 5 cm, internal radius (r) = 4 cm, height (h) = 20 cm. · External curved surface area = 2πRh = 2 X 3.14 X 5 X 20 = 628 cm². · Internal curved surface area = 2πrh = 2 X 3.14 X 4 X 20 = 502.4 cm². · Area of one circular ring (at each end) = π(R² - r²) = 3.14 X (25 - 16) = 3.14 X 9 = 28.26 cm². There are 2 such circular rings (two ends), so total = 2 X 28.26 = 56.52 cm². · Total surface area = external CSA + internal CSA + area of both circular ends = 628 + 502.4 + 56.52 = 1187 approx.

questions.question: The line y = 1/4 x + 2 intersects a line perpendicularly at the point (4, 3). Find the equation of the perpendicular line.
questions.explanation: Slope of given line: m1 = 1/4 Slope of perpendicular line: m2 = -1/m1 = -4 Equation using point-slope form: y - y1 = m (x - x1) y - 3 = -4(x - 4) y - 3 = -4x + 16 y = -4x + 19

questions.question: 2 x 4 ÷ 6_________3 + 2 ÷ 3 x 4 of 4 - 2 x 1_________3 ÷2 + 6 ÷ 3 of 4
questions.explanation: Using the BODMAS rule: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image030.png) of ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image031.png) = of ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image035.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image036.png) of ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image037.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image038.png) of ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image039.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image040.png) of = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image041.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image042.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8346/image032.png)

questions.question: In what ratio should water be mixed with milk costing Rs. 44 per liter to obtain a mixture worth Rs. 32 per liter?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26295/image001.png) Water : Milk = 12 : 32 = 3 : 8

questions.question: In an election between P and Q, every fifth vote polled was marked as invalid by the machine. In the remaining votes, P wins the election with a margin of 3000 votes or 12% of the total votes polled, over Q. If 75% of the invalid votes would have been in favour of Q, then which of the following would have been the result of the election?
questions.explanation: Given, 12% of total votes polled = 3000 100% of total votes polled = 3000 x 100/12 = 25000 Number of invalid votes = 25000/5 = 5000 Number of valid votes = 20000 Given, P wins with a margin of 3000 votes, so the number of votes P gets = 11500, and the number of votes Q gets = 8500 Now, 75% of 5000 = 3750 So, the number of votes Q gets = 8500 + 3750 = 12250 So, Q would have won the election with a margin of 12250 - 11500 = 750 votes. Required percentage = 750 x 100/25000 = 3%

questions.question: The centroid of an equilateral triangle XYZ is W such that XV is the median. If XY = 11 cm, then find the length of WV.
questions.explanation: Median of equilateral triangle = √3 * side/2 = √3 * 11/2 = 11√3/2 cm We know that centroid divides median in the ratio of 2: 1. So, WV = (11√3/2) * 1/3 = 11/2√3 cm

questions.question: The average of three numbers A, B, and C is 15 less than the average of three numbers B, C, and D. Find the value of A, if A: D = 1: 3.
questions.explanation: According to question, (A + B + C)/3 = {(B + C + D)/3} - 15 {(A + B + C)/3} + 15 = (B + C + D)/3 3A + 3B + 3C + 45 * 3 = 3B + 3C + 3D 3D - 3A = 45 * 3 D - A = 45...(1) Let A = X, and D = 3X From equation (1), 3X - X = 45 2X = 45 X = 45/2 So, A = 22(1/2)

questions.question: A book passes through two sellers and is finally sold at a total profit of 22% of its original cost price. If the first seller makes a profit of 13%, what is the profit percentage (correct up to two decimal places) made by the second seller?
questions.explanation: Two consecutive profits% are here. So, we can use successive percentage formula here to find the second profit%, let say A%. 13 + A + (13 x A/100) = 22 113A/100 = 9 A = 900/113 = 7.96%

questions.question: P is 20% and 25% more efficient than Q and R respectively. Q can do 3/5th part of a job in 14.4 days. If P starts work and on every second day R joined him, then in how many days the job will be done?
questions.explanation: Efficiency ratio, P: Q = 120: 100 = 6: 5 Time ratio, P: Q = 5: 6 Efficiency ratio, P: R = 125: 100 = 5: 4 Time ratio, P: R = 4: 5 Now, Q = 14.4 * 5/3 = 24 days P = (5/6) * 24 = 20 days R = (5/4) * 20 = 25 days Two days' work done by P and R together = (2/20 + 1/25) = 7/50 Then, 14 days' work done by P and R together = 7 * 7/50 = 49/50 Remaining work = 1 - 49/50 = 1/50 And, time taken by P to complete remaining work = (1/50) * 20 = 0.4 days Therefore, total time taken = 14 + 0.4 days = 14.4 days

questions.question: Simplify the following - 1_________4 x 11 + 1_________11 x 18 + 1_________18 x 25 + 1_________25 x 32
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24240/image006.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24240/image007.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24240/image008.png) = 1/32

questions.question: In the following figure, ∠ ADB = 40° and ∠ CAB = 50°. Find the measure of ∠ ABC. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21909/image006.png)
questions.explanation: We know that angles in the same segment are equal. So, ∠ ADB = ∠ ACB = 40° In Δ ABC, ∠ ABC + ∠ ACB + ∠ BAC = 180° ∠ ABC + 40 + 50 = 180° ∠ ABC = 90°

questions.question: What is the one-word substitute for the burial of a corpse in a grave or tomb?
questions.explanation: A poem of serious reflection, typically a lament for the dead - "Elegy". A solemn procession, especially for a funeral - "Cortege". Killing of one's wife - "Uxoricide". Option A will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence. You should never trust a stalking horse or else you will regret it later.
options: A horse that doesn't listen to its master
questions.explanation: A stalking horse: a person who pretends; a person or thing that is used to conceal someone's real intentions. It is in context to the fact that we should never trust a person who is pretentious. The rest of the meanings are contextually incorrect. Therefore, the correct answer is:(b) A person who pretends.

questions.question: Pick the spelling that is correct for the act of using your thumbprint/ID to enter an app.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is Authentication; it contains "authen" and ends with "-tication." Rule (simple): Long tech words are built from a base word (authentic) + ending; missing letters makes the word wrong. Example: "Two-factor authentication improves safety." Similar-looking words: authentic, authenticity, authenticate, authentication, author Meaning: Authentication means checking that a user is real/allowed. Synonyms: verification, validation, identity check A: misses an "i" (Authentcation) making it incomplete. C: changes "e" to "a" (Authantication) which is incorrect. D: uses a different ending (Authentification) which is not the standard spelling in common exam usage. Final correct sentence: Two-factor authentication keeps the account safer.

questions.question: According to the passage, why is citizen participation important in scientific research?
options: Scientists lack the necessary training to collect data
Volunteers can replace scientists in research work
Scientists alone cannot gather data from every location
Volunteers provide financial support to research institutions
questions.explanation: It is mentioned in the second paragraph of the passage that "Because scientists alone cannot collect data from every location, the participation of thousands of volunteers significantly increases the amount of information available. Apart from assisting scientific research, citizen science also promotes public awareness and education." Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Identify the type of sentence: "Because scientists alone cannot collect data from every location, the participation of volunteers increases the amount of information available."
questions.explanation: The sentence begins with the subordinate clause "Because scientists alone cannot collect data from every location," which cannot stand alone as a complete sentence. This is followed by the main (independent) clause "the participation of volunteers increases the amount of information available." Since the sentence contains one dependent clause and one independent clause, it is classified as a complex sentence. Simple sentence: Incorrect because the sentence has more than one clause. Compound sentence: Incorrect as it does not contain two independent clauses joined by a conjunction. Imperative sentence: Incorrect because it is not giving a command or instruction. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: The word "widespread" in the passage most nearly means:
questions.explanation: In the passage, the word "widespread" is used in the context of the use of smartphones and technology, indicating that they are used by many people in many places. Thus, it most nearly means "common" or widely prevalent. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. • Scattered: Incorrect because it suggests something spread irregularly, not necessarily widely used. • Frequent: Incorrect as it refers to how often something happens, not how widely it is distributed. • Universal: Incorrect because it means present everywhere without exception, which is stronger than the intended meaning.

questions.question: What does the term "citizen science" refer to in the passage?
options: Scientific research conducted only by professional scientists
Participation of ordinary people in scientific research projects
questions.explanation: The passage clearly defines "citizen science" as the involvement of ordinary people who may not have formal scientific training in scientific research. These individuals contribute by collecting data, making observations, or assisting in analysis, thereby supporting professional scientists. Option (b) accurately reflects this definition. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: According to the passage, what responsibility may participants develop through citizen science?
options: Protecting natural resources and supporting evidence-based decisions
Conducting independent experiments in universities across the world
Publishing scientific journals and witnessing their successful research
questions.explanation: The passage states that participation in citizen science increases public awareness and understanding of scientific and environmental issues. As a result, individuals are encouraged to take greater responsibility for protecting natural resources and making decisions based on scientific evidence. Therefore, option (b) directly reflects the outcome mentioned in the passage. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Choose the sentence with correct collocation for the word 'effort'.
options: She put great effort into completing the project.
She produced great effort into completing the project.
She arranged great effort into completing the project.
She operated great effort into completing the project.
questions.explanation: The correct collocation is "put effort into something", which means to apply energy or hard work toward a task. "Produce effort", "arrange effort", and "operate effort" are not standard collocations in this context. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. My neighbour was admitted to the hospital when he developed an allergic ______ to some medicine he took.
questions.explanation: Let's look at the meanings of the given words. Reaction: an adverse physiological response to a substance that has been breathed in, ingested, or touched. Result: a thing that is caused or produced by something else; a consequence or outcome. Retort: a sharp, angry, or witty reply. Rejoinder: a reply, especially a sharp or witty one. Among the given options, 'reaction' is the most appropriate choice as we commonly use the term 'allergic reaction' to describe the side effects of some medicines. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate degree of comparison. She speaks English ____________ than she did last year.
questions.explanation: The word "than" in the sentence signals that a comparison is being made between two things: how she speaks English now and how she spoke it last year. When comparing two items, the comparative degree must be used. The word needed is an adverb (describing the verb "speaks"). The base adverb is "fluently." For adverbs with two or more syllables, especially those ending in "-ly," the comparative is formed by adding "more" before the adverb. The complete sentence is: She speaks English more fluently than she did last year. Hence, option (a) is the answer. Option b) fluently: This is the positive degree and does not show comparison. Option c) most fluently: This is the superlative degree, used for comparing three or more things (e.g., "She speaks the most fluently in her class"). Option d) fluentlier: This is not a word. Adverbs ending in "-ly" do not form their comparative by adding "-er." Note: · The Positive Degree is used to denote the mere existence of some quality of what we speak about. It is used when no comparison is made. · The Comparative degree denotes a higher degree of quality than the positive and is used when two things are compared. · The Superlative degree indicates the highest degree of quality and is used when more than two things (or sets of things) are compared.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A group of fish
questions.explanation: School - a group of fish Hence, option (d) is the answer. Troop - a group of soldiers Bunch - a group of things Litter - a group of baby cats, dogs, or other animals that are born at the same time.

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence is given which is divided into four parts. Out of the four parts, one part contains an error. Mark that option as your answer. More than one train have (A)/ been delayed due to (B)/ fog today, so you might (C)/ have to wait a little bit.
questions.explanation: Part A has an error. The phrases like 'more than one', 'many a' are singular in form but plural in meaning and take up singular verbs. So, 'have' should be replaced by 'has' in order to rectify the error. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. The final correct sentence would be: More than one train has been delayed due to fog today, so you might have to wait a little bit.

questions.question: Select the sentence that uses the given idiom correctly. Go back to the drawing board
options: He went back to the drawing board when he received maximum marks in the test.
She went back to the drawing board when she finished her painting and hung it on the wall.
He went back to the drawing board when he lost his job and looked for a new one.
She went back to the drawing board when she won the lottery and bought a new house.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) 'He went back to the drawing board when he lost his job and looked for a new one'. The idiom "go back to the drawing board" means to start over or try something again, usually after a failure or a setback. It is usually used to describe situations where someone has to rethink their plans or strategies and come up with a new or better solution. The sentence (c) uses the idiom correctly to show that he had to start looking for a new job after losing his previous one. The other sentences do not use the idiom correctly because they either relate it to something other than a failure or a setback or do not imply that someone is starting over or trying something again.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Your's truly, honoured to be of service and eager to assist in any way possible, extends sincere regards and remains faithfully dedicated to supporting your needs and aspirations.
questions.explanation: An apostrophe is not used with possessive pronouns (his, hers, yours, ours, theirs). Example- Yours faithfully, yours truly. The correct sentence is - "Yours truly, honoured to be of service and eager to assist in any way possible, extends sincere regards and remains faithfully dedicated to supporting your needs and aspirations." Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error. Riya completed the task / faster than / any student / in her class.
questions.explanation: When a comparison is instituted by means of a Comparative followed by than, the thing compared must always be excluded from the class of things with which it is compared, by using other or some such words. Example: Priya is wiser than any other student in the class. Correct sentence: Riya completed the task faster than any other student in her class. So, there is an error in the part mentioned in option (a). Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Which among the following sentences uses the adjective with the correct degree of comparison?
options: Of all the performers, his act was the more memorable.
Of all the performers, his act was a most memorable.
Of all the performers, his act was the most memorable.
Of all the performers, his act was the memorablest.
questions.explanation: The adjective "memorable" has three syllables, so its comparative and superlative forms are made with "more" and "most." · Option (a): Incorrect. "More memorable" is the comparative degree, used for comparing two acts. Since the comparison is among "all the performers," the superlative degree is required. · Option (b): Incorrect. The superlative degree requires the definite article "the," not "a." · Option (c): Correct. The superlative form "most memorable" is correctly used to compare one act to the entire group of performers, and it is preceded by the correct article "the." · Option (d): Incorrect. We do not add "-est" to form the superlative of long adjectives like "memorable." Hence, option (c) is the correct answer. Note: · The Positive Degree is used to denote the mere existence of some quality of what we speak about. It is used when no comparison is made. · The Comparative degree denotes a higher degree of quality than the positive and is used when two things are compared. · The Superlative degree indicates the highest degree of quality and is used when more than two things (or sets of things) are compared. Definite and indefinite articles: In the English language, we have one definite article 'the'; it refers to or introduces, a particular, specific noun. 'An' and 'a' are indefinite articles; they refer to, or introduce, an unspecified noun.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Parsimonious
questions.explanation: Parsimonious means very unwilling to spend money or use resources; stingy or frugal. For example, "The parsimonious man refused to heat his own house in winter." Lavish means sumptuously rich, elaborate, or luxurious; spending or giving in great amounts. For example, "She was known for giving lavish parties that were the talk of the town." This generosity and free-spending nature is the opposite of being stingy, making Lavish the correct antonym. The other options - Frugal, Miserly, and Thrifty are all synonyms or near-synonyms of parsimonious, relating to the careful or reluctant use of money. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM to replace the italicised word. It was an arduous seven-hour journey to get there.
questions.explanation: Arduous- full of difficulties; needing a lot of effort. Gruelling- very tiring and long So, the two words can be used as synonyms of each other. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Recommendations for the Bharat Ratna are made by the Prime Minister with a maximum of three nominees being awarded per year. B. The award was originally limited to achievements in the arts, literature, science and public services. C. 'Bharat Ratna' is India's highest award given to a civilian. D. But the government expanded the criteria to include 'any field of human endeavour' in December 2011.
questions.explanation: Sentence C comes first as it introduces us to the main subject of the passage (Bharat Ratna). Sentence B talks about the initial categories that were considered for Bharat Ratna. Sentence D starts with 'but' which is used to introduce a phrase or clause contrasting with what has already been mentioned. D states that the government expanded the criteria to include one more field of achievement. So, BD is a pair. Sentence A can be placed either in the second position or as a concluding sentence of the passage. None of the options contains A in the second position. So, it could be the last sentence of the passage. Therefore, CBDA becomes the correct order among the given options. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the word almost nearest in meaning to the word given below. Dismantle
questions.explanation: Dismantle - to take a machine apart or to come apart into separate pieces. Disassemble - take (something) to pieces. So, both words are similar in meaning. Hence, option (d) is the answer. Reserve - retain for future use. Preserve - maintain (something) in its original or existing state. Reconstruct - build or form (something) again after it has been damaged or destroyed.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate synonym of Irksome:
questions.explanation: 'Irksome' means 'Irritating; annoying.'. Options a) and b) can be rejected as they are unrelated to the word. 'Cheerful' means 'Noticeably happy and optimistic.'. Hence, option d) can also be rejected as it is the exact opposite. 'Stentorian' means '(of a person's voice) loud and powerful.'. 'Vacillate' means 'Waver between different opinions or actions; be indecisive.'. Hence, option c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Deconstruct
questions.explanation: 'Deconstruct' means to analyse or dismantle a complex idea, structure, or system into its constituent parts in order to understand its underlying assumptions, biases, or contradictions. 'Synthesize' means to combine or blend different elements or ideas to form a coherent whole. So, the two words can be used as antonyms of each other. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Scrutinise: To examine closely or inspect carefully, often in a critical manner. It implies a thorough and detailed investigation. Probe: To investigate or explore thoroughly, often in order to uncover information or gain understanding. It suggests a systematic inquiry into a subject. Dissect: To analyse or examine something methodically by breaking it down into its component parts. It involves separating and examining individual elements in detail.

questions.question: Pick the spelling that is correct for being awake and aware of one's surroundings.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is b) Conscious. "Conscious" describes the state of being aware of and responsive to one's surroundings.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. Sam said, "I had a great time at the party."
options: Sam said that he had had a great time at the party.
Sam said that he had a great time at the party.
Sam said that he was having a great time at the party.
Sam said that he has had a great time at the party.
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Direct Speech is in Past Perfect. Therefore, the sentence in the Indirect Speech should also be in Past Perfect. Past Perfect form (Subject + had + V3 + Object) should be kept intact in indirect speech. For example: He said, "I had just finished the movie." (Direct Speech) He said that he had just finished the movie. (Indirect Speech) The following thing in the sentence should change when changed from Direct speech to Indirect Speech: "I" changes to "he". Therefore, the correct answer is: "Sam said that he had a great time at the party." Hence, option (b) is correct.
